name: Lemon-Ginger Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Position rack in the center of the oven.
2. Lightly oil a 9-inch springform pan.
3. Finely grind all wafers, gingersnaps, sugar, and lemon peel in a food processor until combined.
4. Add butter and blend well. Sprinkle crumbs over the bottom of the prepared pan. Press to form a buttered crust.
5. Bake until golden brown (about 12 minutes). Cool before filling.
6. For the filling:
- Sprinkle gelatin over cold water in a small bowl. Let stand for 10 minutes to soften.
- In a food processor, finely mince lemon zest, ginger, sugar, and salt until all are as fine as sugar.
- Add egg yolks and blend until light and fluffy.
- Scald milk in a heavy saucepan. With the blender running, add milk through the feed tube and blend well.
- Return mixture to the saucepan. Stir over medium heat until thickened (about 12 minutes). Do not boil.
- Add gelatin mixture to custard and stir until dissolved.
- Refrigerate until thickened but not set (about 20 minutes), stirring often.
7. In a large bowl, blend cream cheese and lemon juice until smooth. Add the custard from the fridge and blend until smooth.
8. Whip whipping cream in a medium bowl until it holds soft peaks. Gently fold into the custard filling.
9. Pour half of the filling over the crust. Spoon half of the lemon curd by spoonfuls over the filling. Swirl mixtures together using the tip of a knife.
10. Pour remaining filling over. Spoon remaining curd over and repeat swirls.
11. Refrigerate at least 4 hours or overnight.
12. Run a knife around the sides of the cake. Release the pan.
Lemon Curd can be bought at specialty stores or check the Gourmet section.
